title: ANIMA Knowledge Card
description: One unit of ANIMA's retrieval knowledge (RAG). The device embeds the user's query and cosine-matches it against the `ask` phrasings of every card; the best match above the confidence gate returns this card's frozen `reply`. Knowledge lives in these cards, never in the model. Authored as one JSON object per line (JSONL) under tools/anima/knowledge/*.jsonl, then compiled into anima-it-index.bin by tools/anima/build_akb2.py.

id.description: Stable unique identifier (dotted/kebab), e.g. geo.capital.francia. Used for dedup and provenance.

category.description: Knowledge domain / kind. Drives the source file and lets the corpus stay modular. Cards learned online (docs/anima-online.md) are auto-classified from the source's one-line description into a KIND so they're filed correctly, never a generic bucket: person | place | organization | work | species | event | concept.

action.description: What the device should do when this card wins. 'answer' just speaks the reply; 'launch' opens the app named in arg; 'system' fills {value} with a live reading named in arg (time, storage).

arg.description: Argument for the action: app id for 'launch', reading name for 'system', empty for 'answer'.

reply.description: The frozen response. Provide both languages when you can; a single-language card is allowed and the compiler mirrors it to the other language. May contain {value} for system actions.

detail.description: Optional drill-down text returned on a 'tell me more' / 'dimmi di più' follow-up about this card (two-level retrieval, no generation). Longer than reply: a worked example, the gotchas, the rearranged formula. Omit it and the assistant honestly says it has nothing more.

ask.description: Example ways a user might ask for this card, per language. These are what the retriever matches against — more varied phrasings = better recall. 2-6 per language is a good target.

source.description: Optional provenance, e.g. 'curated' or 'book:Atlante p.12'. Helps audit and re-ingest.

lang_primary.description: Language the card was authored in; 'bi' when both replies are first-class. Build-side only (not shipped to the device).

tags.description: Free-form labels for filtering/curation (build-side only, not shipped to the device).

last_updated.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the last edit. Build-side only; useful for audits and staged re-ingest.

ttl_days.description: Freshness window for online-learned cards (docs/anima-online.md §6). Past last_updated + ttl_days the device silently re-fetches when online; offline it serves the cached value with its date. Stable entities use a long TTL; volatile facts a short one. Curated cards omit it.
